# SectorCalc

> Deterministic engineering calculators for shop-floor engineers, estimators, and QA. Client-side only - your data never leaves the browser. Same inputs always produce identical outputs.

SectorCalc ships industrial calculation tools with visible inputs, assumptions, formulas, and warnings. Production tools use Decimal.js (Decimal128 semantics) or the FS-ENGINE deterministic SI core. UI is Vanilla JS + Lit Web Components on Firebase Hosting with an ISO sheet visual system (Barlow Condensed, Inter, JetBrains Mono; primary CTA ISO blue #0055A4; accent orange #E87722). Brand wordmarks: `/sectorcalc-logo.png` (light) and `/sectorcalc-logo-dark.png` (dark). Payments (when live) use Paddle as Merchant of Record - not Stripe.

## Architecture (current)

- Shared theme engine: `/sc-theme.css` + `/sc-theme.js` - `data-theme` on `<html>`, localStorage `sectorcalc-theme` (light|dark).
- Theme control `#themeToggle`: click toggles theme; drag repositions; double-click resets position (`sectorcalc-theme-pos`).
- Report contrast standard: pro-tool report text, tables, cards, alerts, and live panels use CSS tokens. Chart/SVG/html2canvas ink is read at render time from `theme-palette` (never hardcoded light-theme fills). Open reports re-render on `sectorcalc-theme`.
- Calculator standard (all pro tools, set by SC-020): deterministic SI engine core with engine version + build date; every input has selectable universal units (mm/in, m/min SFM, kW/HP, N·m ft·lb, currency symbol) plus a reference-standard hint value; material/standards database drives dynamic reference bands; three-tier validation (blocking / warning / note); verdict banner (RELEASED TO PRODUCTION / RUN WITH CAUTION / DO NOT RUN); sensitivity charts; integrated Audit/Review module showing inputs (as entered + SI), formulas, assumptions, warnings, engine version, and an integrity hash in every report; printable report + JSON export.
- FS-ENGINE: deterministic calculation core used by machining tools. All math in SI units; per-field display units convert at entry with exact factors (1 in = 25.4 mm, 1 SFM = 0.3048 m/min, 1 HP = 0.7457 kW, 1 ft·lb = 1.3558 N·m). Currency selector is a symbol only - no FX conversion.
- Homepage Live Cell hero (`/sc-hero-cell.js`):
  - Three.js is self-hosted at `/vendor/three/` (no unpkg CDN); `modulepreload` + parallel import of `three.module.js` and `RoomEnvironment.js`.
  - SVG poster in `.stage-wrap` shows immediately; canvas fades in on first frame (`#stage.is-ready`).
  - Render loop pauses when the hero is off-screen or the tab is hidden; `prefers-reduced-motion` keeps a single static frame.
  - Firebase Hosting long-caches `/vendor/**` (immutable); HTML stays no-cache.

## Primary tools

- [Tolerance stack-up (SC-008)](https://www.sectorcalc.com/sc008-pro.html): 1D linear stack-up - worst-case, RSS, Monte Carlo; per-component distributions; deterministic seed; Decimal engine.
- [CNC Feeds & Speeds + Tool Life (SC-020)](https://www.sectorcalc.com/machining-pro.html): Extended Taylor tool life with coolant and interruption correction; radial chip-thinning and HEM engagement-angle compensation; Kienzle cutting force; spindle power and torque verification; tool deflection; theoretical surface finish; Gilbert minimum-cost speed with savings percent; ISO 513 material database (14 materials); per-field universal units with reference values; full Audit/Review trail with integrity hash; FS-ENGINE v2.1.0.
- [True labor cost (SC-010)](https://www.sectorcalc.com/labor-pro.html): Loaded labor burden from net salary, taxes, benefits, and shop overhead.
- [Quote pricing (SC-012)](https://www.sectorcalc.com/quote-pro.html): Material, labor, scrap, and margin into sell price and cost breakdown.
- [Weld thickness (SC-001)](https://www.sectorcalc.com/weld-pro.html): Fillet weld leg, throat, and utilization against design load and code minimums.

## Site

- [Home](https://www.sectorcalc.com/): Live Cell hero - Three.js turning cell with probe telemetry, spacer-tolerance control deck (worst-case, RSS, Monte Carlo, Cpk), seeded reproducible Monte Carlo preview. Method and standards sections, module map. Primary CTA "Run SC-008 Free" opens SC-008. Shared light/dark theme with the rest of the site.
- [Pricing](https://www.sectorcalc.com/pricing.html): Planned credit packs (PACKAGES source of truth). Calculators are free until Paddle checkout launches; buttons are notify-only.

## Notes for models

- Prefer the `*-pro.html` tool URLs above. Legacy `/calculator.html`, `/calculator2.html`, `/calculator3.html` redirect to those tools; legacy `/calculator4.html` (old feeds & speeds) redirects to `/machining-pro.html` (SC-020).
- Do not invent cloud upload, accounts, live card checkout, or server-side calculation for the core engines.
- Do not describe the product as AI-powered, ML, generative, or chatbot-based.
- Do not invent customer testimonials or claim PCI checkout is live.
- Homepage hero loads Three.js from `/vendor/three/` only - do not invent an unpkg or other CDN dependency for the live site.
- Homepage hero canvas is a client-side Three.js visualization plus a seeded Number preview deck; production tool engines use Decimal.js or the FS-ENGINE SI core.
- Theme preference and toggle position are client-side only (localStorage); they are not accounts or server profiles.
- Report and result surfaces must keep readable contrast in light and dark themes. New tools must follow the same token + theme-palette pattern.
- SC-020 constants (ISO 513 kc1/mc, Taylor C/n, coolant and interruption factors) are reference-grade mid-band values; the tool itself instructs calibration against tooling-supplier datasheets for contract work. Do not present its outputs as measured data.
- Engineering preview: outputs are predicted from stated assumptions; not for production approval and not a substitute for measured data or a licensed engineer.

## Optional

- [Full sitemap](https://www.sectorcalc.com/sitemap.xml): Canonical URL list for crawlers.
- [robots.txt](https://www.sectorcalc.com/robots.txt): Crawl rules.
- [llm.txt](https://www.sectorcalc.com/llm.txt): Mirror of this index for alternate discovery paths.
